fix: use relative path prefix ./ in marketplace.json plugin source

The Claude Code marketplace schema requires relative paths to start with
"./" — bare paths like "plugins/agent-backlog" fail schema validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add dynamic searchable filters to sales forecasting with aggregation-level based UI

This commit implements a comprehensive dynamic filter system that changes based on the selected aggregation level, with searchable dropdowns powered by Choices.js.

Key Features:
- Dynamic filter panel that shows different filters based on aggregation level:
  * By School: School filter dropdown
  * By Product: Product name + Style code filter dropdowns
  * By Shop: Shop location filter dropdown
  * By Category: Category filter dropdown
- Searchable dropdowns with enhanced UX (Choices.js integration)
- Data loads only on "Apply Filters" button click (no default loading)
- Backend API endpoint for filter options: /dashboard/forecasting/filter-options/
- Filter logic integrated into forecast queries for all aggregation levels

Backend Changes:
- Add forecasting_filter_options() API view to return filter data
- Modify sales_forecasting() view to skip data loading on initial page load
- Implement filter parameter extraction and application in queries
- Add SQL JOIN logic for product-level filtering across dimensions
- Update cache keys to include filter parameters

Frontend Changes:
- Replace Quick Select section with Advanced Filters panel
- Add Choices.js library integration with custom styling
- Implement dynamic filter visibility based on aggregation level
- Add AJAX loading of filter options when level changes
- Enhanced CSS for selected item visibility (blue background + checkmark)

Fixes:
- Fix Choices.js double initialization error (removed data-choices attributes)
- Prevent default data loading on page load (require explicit filter application)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: mint UUID7 request_id for internally-triggered sessions in spawner [bu-0b7.5] (#493)

* fix: mint UUID7 request_id for internally-triggered sessions in spawner [bu-0b7.5]

Satisfies the non-null request_id contract on session_create() for all
call-site patterns:

- Connector-sourced sessions (route/external): the pipeline-provided
  request_id is passed through unchanged.
- Internally-triggered sessions (tick, scheduler, manual trigger): a
  fresh UUID7 is minted via generate_uuid7_string() before session_create()
  is called. ingestion_event_id is not applicable for these sessions.

No change to the Spawner.trigger() or daemon API surface — this is
purely internal to _run().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct misleading 'UUID4 pattern' comment to 'UUID pattern'

The regex validates any UUID string format (version-agnostic), not specifically
UUID4. The comment was copied incorrectly; this PR is about UUID7 minting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: harden input validation and system prompt against adversarial inputs (#21)

Discovered via LocalStack adversarial testing (31 attacks across prompt
injection, SQL injection, illicit asks, malformed inputs, and resource
abuse). The existing SQL injection and user isolation defenses held, but
input validation at the handler boundary had gaps.

Handler validation:
- Reject empty/whitespace/non-string questions
- Enforce max question length (2000 chars) to prevent token waste
- Reject user_id <= 0 for integer-typed DBs
- Reject empty/non-string user_id for string-typed DBs
- Enforce max session_id length (128 chars)
- Reject empty/non-string session_id

System prompt hardening:
- Anti-prompt-injection: ignore contradictory instructions
- Refuse off-topic, illegal, and code-generation requests
- Never reveal system prompt, tool names, or DB structure
- Never discuss or generate raw SQL

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: lägg till timeout på Groq-anrop och gör ljuduppladdning icke-blockerande

- transcription.js: 90s AbortController timeout på transcribe(), 60s på
  detectTranscriptionLanguage() – appen hänger sig inte längre om Groq är trög
- transcription.js: transcribe() returnerar null vid fel/timeout och '' vid

commit.message:
docs(compiler): document untestable TypeAssertionExpression path (#1002)

* docs(compiler): document untestable TypeAssertionExpression path [#988]

Angle-bracket type assertions (<Type>expr) are invalid in .tsx files —
the parser treats <Type> as JSX. Since components must be in .tsx files,
this unwrapTypeWrappers path is unreachable in practice but handled
defensively. Added a comment explaining why there's no test.

Tests angle-bracket type assertions (<Type>expr) which are only valid
in .ts files. Uses a manually constructed ComponentInfo to bypass
ComponentAnalyzer's JSX requirement, exercising the full analyzer path
with a .ts source file.

commit.message:
Fix: Bump localStorage version to apply new page size default

**Problem**: Users who previously set page size to 10 were not seeing the
new default of 50 because their old preference persisted in localStorage.

**Solution**:
- Bumped `talentpool_page_size_v1` → `v2` to invalidate old preferences
- All users will now get the new 50-per-page default
- No cache clearing required by customers

**Documentation**: Added LOCALSTORAGE_VERSIONING.md with best practices
for versioning localStorage keys to prevent this issue in the future.

Merge branch 'claude/sharp-meninsky': zsxq_index.py — PDF indexer with AI classification

Adds zsxq_index.py, a companion to zsxq_downloader.py that indexes all
PDFs from the zsxq group into a local SQLite database (zsxq.db) with
full summaries, metadata, and AI/Robotics classification.

Features:
- Full pagination via end_time cursor (handles 2000+ files)
- Extracts un-truncated topic titles from talk.text (API title is capped)
- Stores complete raw topic JSON (likes, comments, reads, etc.)
- --last-x-files N: index only the N most recent files (single API call for N≤20)
- --classify: calls MiniMax (MiniMax-Text-01) to classify each PDF as
  AI/Robotics-related; reads API key from config.py automatically
- Auto-downloads AI/Robotics-related PDFs to the local downloads folder
  and updates local_path + downloaded_at in the DB and tracker JSON
- Retry/back-off for transient zsxq API errors (code 1059)
- UPSERT logic + safe ALTER TABLE migrations for schema evolution

commit.message:
feat: add inference module and FastAPI serving layer

src/predict.py
- load_model: deserialises best_model.pkl; raises FileNotFoundError / RuntimeError
- build_feature_row: converts raw dict → one-row DataFrame matching training schema
  (applies same one-hot encoding for loan_purpose, computes engineered features)
- risk_level: maps probability → LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH (thresholds 0.30 / 0.60)
- predict: single-application inference returning prediction, probability, risk tier

api/main.py
- GET  /health  → HealthResponse: status, model_loaded, version, uptime_seconds
- POST /predict → PredictionResponse: prediction, probability, risk_level, model_version
- GET  /metrics → MetricsResponse: f1, auc_roc, precision, recall (from env vars)
- LoanFeatures Pydantic model: field-level bounds + loan_purpose validator (422 on bad input)
- HTTP middleware: logs every request with method, path, status, elapsed ms;
  adds X-Response-Time-Ms response header
- Global exception handler: converts unhandled errors to structured 500 JSON
- Model loaded once at startup (avoids per-request disk I/O, keeps p99 < 100 ms)
- /metrics reads MODEL_METRIC_* env vars set at deploy time; returns 503 if absent
- Full OpenAPI descriptions on every endpoint, tag grouping (Inference / Operations)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Cache-Control: no-cache to SPA index.html response

Without this header, browsers can cache the HTML page indefinitely,
serving stale script tags that point to old JS bundle hashes even
after a production deploy with new content-hashed assets.

commit.message:
feat: implement JSON operators for JSON/JSONB types

Implemented 7 JSON operators with full functionality:
- -> (json_extract): Extract JSON field as JSON
- ->> (json_extract_text): Extract JSON field as text
- @> (json_contains): Check if left JSON contains right JSON
- ? (json_key_exists): Check if JSON has key
- ?| (json_any_key_exists): Check if JSON has any of the keys
- ?& (json_all_keys_exist): Check if JSON has all of the keys
- #> (json_path_extract): Extract JSON by path array
- #- (json_delete_path): Delete path from JSON (single-level only)

Implementation details:
- Uses std.json.parseFromSlice for JSON parsing
- Uses std.json.fmt for JSON serialization (Zig 0.15 API)
- JSON/JSONB stored as text/blob (no .json variant in Value enum)
- ArrayListUnmanaged pattern for Zig 0.15 compatibility
- Added 18 comprehensive unit tests covering all operators

Test status: 1332/1335 passing (3 test failures to fix):
- Old "not yet implemented" test needs removal
- Minor test output mismatches in extract/delete tests

feat(storage): PostgreSQL full-text search via tsvector + BM25 abstraction fixes (#24)

* feat(storage): add PostgreSQL full-text search via tsvector + fix BM25 abstraction violations

- Add search_vector tsvector column + GIN indexes to documents and memories tables
  in PostgreSQL backend (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS migrations)
- Populate search_vector on every document/memory insert/upsert using app-side
  tokenizeCode/tokenizeCodeWithAST/tokenizeDocs (same tokenizers as SQLite BM25)
- Add hybridSearchDocuments, hybridSearchMemories, hybridSearchGlobalMemories methods
  to PostgresBackend — two queries (ts_rank_cd text + pgvector cosine), fused via RRF
- Add updateDocumentSearchVector, updateMemorySearchVector helpers for dispatcher
- Fix dispatcher/search.ts: all 8 BM25 methods now properly branch on PG backend
  - 5 invalidate* methods: early return on PG (tsvector is maintained on insert)
  - 3 update* methods: route to new PG search-vector helpers
  - 4 hybrid search methods: use new PG hybrid search instead of vector-only fallback
- Fix storage abstraction violation: src/lib/bpe.ts directly imported db/bpe.js;
  add explanatory comment documenting the intentional SQLite-only guard
- Strengthen raw-sql-audit.test.ts: add DB_SUBMODULE_IMPORT_RE to catch db/* imports
  that bypass the storage abstraction; allowlist src/lib/bpe.ts with comment
- Document search capabilities per backend in docs/storage.md (Search Capabilities
  section + PostgreSQL Pros bullet for full hybrid search)

commit.message:
Fix _ct fixture to reuse ct module and use object-based monkeypatch

_ct was independently loading classify_test, creating a separate module
object from ct. This caused stub_classifier and stub_side_effects to
patch one module while tests used another. Fix by having _ct delegate
to ct via request.getfixturevalue.

Also replace string-based monkeypatch.setattr calls in test_github.py
with object-based calls using the ct_github fixture directly, since
string-based resolution requires proper package parent-child
relationships that module_loader does not establish.
